videos.title: Security in Springboot | Chai aur Springboot
videos.description:
Visit https://chaicode.com
in Paid Collab with  @TechByAniOfficial 

0:00 Spring Boot Security Overview
2:59 Handling Exceptions in Spring Boot
7:38 Centralized Exception Handling
21:59 Validation in Spring Boot
41:21 Introduction to Spring Security
46:32 Security Filter Chain Explained
52:40 Implementing Spring Security
58:49 Configuring Security in Spring Boot
1:15:38 JWT vs Session Authentication
1:36:01 Dependencies for JWT Implementation
1:39:23 Creating Auth Controller
1:53:25 Public Auth Requests Configuration
2:32:41 Verifying JWT Tokens
2:45:53 Role-Based Access Control
2:59:01 Summary and Learnings

Dive into the intricate world of Spring Boot security and exception handling with this comprehensive guide. Designed for those who have ventured into the third installment of the 'Tea and Spring Boot' series, this video takes you through the essential security measures needed in today's AI-driven world. As security becomes paramount, understanding the foundational elements like exception handling is crucial for safeguarding applications.

The session begins by delving into the basics of exception handling in Spring Boot. You'll explore how unexpected events, such as a missing user in the database, can disrupt the program flow and learn to manage these exceptions effectively. The video illustrates the creation of a custom exception, demonstrating how to provide meaningful error responses using DTOs and centralized exception handlers. This approach ensures a clean, standardized, and user-friendly error management process.

Moving forward, the focus shifts to Spring Security. You’ll gain insights into how Spring Security addresses the challenges of traditional security methods by introducing a centralized, filter-based framework. The video explains the role of the security filter chain, detailing how various filters like CSRF, JWT token, and authentication filters work together to protect your application from unauthorized access. Additionally, the session covers the implementation of JWT for stateless session management, highlighting the advantages of using tokens over maintaining server-side sessions.

By the end of this tutorial, you'll be equipped with the knowledge to implement robust security measures in your Spring Boot applications, ensuring they are well-protected against potential threats. Whether it's handling exceptions gracefully or securing APIs with JWT and filter chains, you'll have the tools needed to enhance the security and reliability of your projects.

videos.title: Building RESTAPI and Database details in springboot | Chai aur Springboot
videos.description:
Visit https://chaicode.com 
in Paid Collab with  @TechByAniOfficial 

0:01 Spring Boot Series Introduction
2:09 Writing REST APIs in Spring Boot
5:00 Understanding REST API Basics
12:02 Setting Up Project Dependencies
17:17 Creating In-Memory Data for Testing
25:47 Testing REST API with Postman
34:06 HTTP Status Codes in REST APIs
39:56 Creating User with POST Mapping
51:17 Updating User with PUT Mapping
59:40 Deleting User with DELETE Mapping
1:05:33 Java Persistence Evolution Overview
1:17:57 Setting Up Spring Data JPA
1:21:38 Running Postgres with Docker
1:41:25 Creating Tables with Hibernate
2:07:04 Building REST API for Data Operations
2:30:45 Handling User Operations in Controller
2:57:02 Order Management in Spring Boot
3:28:07 Implementing Paging and Sorting

Learn the intricate process of developing REST APIs using Spring Boot in this comprehensive tutorial. This video is part of the 'Tea and Spring Boot' series, where we delve deep into the core concepts and practical applications of REST API development. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this video provides valuable insights into creating robust APIs that can seamlessly communicate between client and server.

The journey begins with a foundational understanding of REST APIs, explaining their role as a contract for communication between software systems. The tutorial then transitions into building APIs using Spring Boot, emphasizing the importance of understanding the flow of RESTful services. You'll learn about various HTTP methods such as GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, and PATCH, and how they interact with resources like users and orders. The video also covers the nuances of handling data without direct database connectivity, using in-memory data structures to simulate database operations.

As the tutorial progresses, you'll explore the setup of a Spring Boot project with IntelliJ, including setting up dependencies and creating packages for controllers, DTOs, and services. The practical segment demonstrates how to create endpoints, manage data using repositories, and implement business logic in service layers. Additionally, the video provides a detailed explanation of HTTP status codes and how to handle them in your API responses.

By the end of this tutorial, you'll have the knowledge and skills to build efficient REST APIs using Spring Boot, understand the importance of design patterns in API development, and be able to implement pagination, sorting, and data validation in your projects.

videos.title: Foundation of Spring Boot | Chai aur Springboot
videos.description:
For more visit: https://chaicode.com

in Paid Collab with @TechByAniOfficial 

0:08 Welcome to Chai or Code
3:06 Introduction to Spring Boot
4:47 Why Spring Boot?
13:40 Spring Boot Configuration
17:07 Prerequisites for Spring Boot
18:21 Using IntelliJ IDEA for Development
21:48 Project Setup and Metadata
25:58 Starting with Spring Web
29:54 Understanding Project Structure
38:22 Building a Basic User Service
51:07 Introduction to Spring Core
1:09:55 Spring Profiles and Configuration
1:17:09 Bean Scopes and Lifecycle
1:25:55 Spring Boot Application Lifecycle
1:44:33 Writing REST APIs in Spring Boot

Embark on a journey to master Spring Boot and revolutionize your Java backend development. This comprehensive series, 'Chai or Spring Boot,' is designed to take you from the basics of Spring and Spring Boot to creating robust, production-ready applications. With only four videos, this series promises to deliver a deep understanding of the Spring Boot framework and its applications.

Starting with the fundamentals, you'll explore the differences between Spring and Spring Boot, understanding why Spring Boot was introduced as an enhancement to the Spring framework. The series delves into key concepts such as dependency injection, event-driven architecture, and microservices. You'll get hands-on with tools like Kafka, Redis, and Spring Security, ensuring you have a rounded knowledge of building secure and scalable Java backends.

The tutorial takes you through the setup process, including using IntelliJ IDEA for development, and covers the intricacies of Spring Boot's auto-configuration and dependency management. You'll learn how to efficiently manage dependencies using Maven and Gradle, and explore the powerful features of Spring Boot's Actuator for monitoring and managing your applications.

By the end of this series, you will be equipped to build and manage complex Java backend systems with confidence, leveraging the full potential of Spring Boot to streamline your development process. Whether you're aiming to create microservices or integrate with cloud technologies, this series provides the essential skills and knowledge to succeed.

videos.title: Stop AI from Ruining Your Code (Do This Simple Tweak!)
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 The Problem with AI Code Suggestions
01:33 Opening Keyboard Shortcuts JSON
02:16 Configuring the Tab Keybinding
02:55 Testing Line-by-Line AI Suggestions
04:17 How to Snooze AI Code Suggestions

Are AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot or Cursor taking over your screen and dumping huge blocks of code all at once? While AI auto-complete is incredibly powerful, accepting entire functions or components with a single 'Tab' press can ruin the learning experience for beginners and make coding tutorials impossible for instructors. If you want to understand what you're building or teach effectively, you need more control.

In this quick and practical tutorial, I will show you exactly how to tame your AI code assistant so it suggests code line-by-line instead of all at once. We dive into the VS Code or Cursor settings, open up the keybindings.json file, and implement a custom keyboard shortcut overriding the default 'Tab' behavior. By mapping 'Tab' to the 'acceptNextLine' command, you regain full control over your code generation.

This simple tweak is an absolute game-changer for coding instructors who want to discuss code step-by-step and developers who want to maintain control over their codebase without being overwhelmed by AI suggestions. I'll also show you a quick tip on how to easily snooze AI suggestions when you just want to write pure manual code.

If you found this coding tip helpful, please give the video a thumbs up, leave a comment if you have any questions, and subscribe for more developer productivity hacks and coding tutorials!

videos.title: JavaScript Module Pattern & Dependency Injection Tutorial
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction to Uncomfy JS
00:48 The 3 Stages of Learning JavaScript
03:25 The Kirana Store Analogy
05:01 Understanding IIFE
06:05 Creating Private Variables & Public Methods
11:17 Example 2: Account Book & Access Logs
20:41 Simulating Modules & Dependency Injection
28:57 Inspecting Module APIs
30:38 Final Thoughts

Welcome to a brand new series: Uncomfy JavaScript! In this video, we dive deep into the rarely touched, foundational concepts of JavaScript that are often used in building libraries, frameworks, and maintaining legacy codebases. If you've mastered JS basics and built some projects, this is your next step.

We'll explore the classic Module Pattern using IIFEs (Immediately Invoked Function Expressions) to create private variables and public APIs—a crucial skill for any senior JS developer. To make things easy, we use a highly relatable 'Kirana Store' analogy to understand how a store's public front differs from its private godown.

Later in the video, we take it a step further and simulate ES6 modules and basic Dependency Injection entirely from scratch in Vanilla JavaScript. We build a 'SharmaMart' consisting of an Inventory module and a BillingCalculator that depends on it. This will give you a solid grasp of how modern JS frameworks manage dependencies under the hood.

Drop a comment below and let me know how much JavaScript you've learned so far! If you enjoy this deep dive into the 'uncomfy' parts of JS, let me know if you'd like more videos in this series.

videos.title: Intern DELETED a Git Branch! How to Save Your Code in 2 Mins
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 The Intern's Git Disaster Scenario
00:50 Setting up the Project & Initial Commits
02:00 Creating a Feature Branch for Payment
04:00 The Mistake: Accidentally Deleting the Branch
05:10 The Solution: Understanding Git Reflog
07:07 Step-by-Step: Recovering the Deleted Branch
08:58 Conclusion & How to Avoid This

Have you ever been in a situation where you or an intern accidentally deleted a crucial Git branch? The panic sets in when you realize hours of code might be gone forever. But don't worry, Git is incredibly forgiving if you know the right commands!

In this episode of our tricky Git situations series, we dive into a classic developer nightmare: accidentally running 'git branch -D' and wiping out an unmerged feature branch. I'll walk you through a real-world scenario where an intern creates a payment integration feature, commits the code, and then disastrously deletes the branch while back on the master branch.

We will explore why standard commands like 'git log' won't help you in this scenario and introduce you to the ultimate lifesaver command: 'git reflog'. You'll learn exactly how Git tracks every single action you take in your repository, maintaining an independent log of your HEAD pointer movements even when branches are removed.

Watch step-by-step as we use 'git reflog' to track down the exact hash of our lost commit and magically resurrect the deleted branch with a simple 'git checkout' command. Whether you are a junior developer, an intern prone to mistakes, or a senior engineer rescuing your team, understanding how to recover deleted branches is a must-have skill in your version control toolkit.

Drop a comment below if you've ever faced a similar Git disaster, and let us know what other complex Git scenarios you want us to solve next! If this trick saved your codebase, make sure to like the video and subscribe for more coding tutorials.

videos.title: The BIGGEST Git Mistake Every Intern Makes (And How to Fix It!)
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction to Real-World Git Situations
02:34 Setting Up the Workspace & Repository
04:12 The Mistake: Committing to the Master Branch
07:18 Realizing the Error & Checking Git Logs
09:12 Switching to the Correct Feature Branch
10:14 Using Git Cherry-Pick to Move Commits
11:06 Cleaning Up Master with Git Reset Hard
12:54 Final Verification & Conclusion

Are you tired of making silly mistakes in Git? Have you ever accidentally committed all your hard work to the master or main branch instead of your feature branch? Don't panic! In this video, we kick off a brand new series focused entirely on real-world Git situations and how to escape them safely. We tackle one of the most common Git mess-ups that interns and junior developers make on the job. We will walk through a complete, hands-on scenario where code is accidentally written on the wrong branch and show you exactly how to fix it without losing any of your precious work. You will learn the practical, step-by-step workflow to safely copy your work over, including how to read your commit history with `git log`, how to safely move individual commits using `git cherry-pick` (and why the order matters!), and finally, how to clean up and restore your master branch to its pristine state using `git reset --hard`. This comprehensive Git tutorial in Hindi is designed to save you from those heart-stopping moments at work and give you absolute control over your codebase. Whether you are preparing for your first internship, starting a new job, or just want to strengthen your situational Git skills to become a better developer, this video has got you covered. The target for this video is just 100 comments! Let me know in the comments if you have ever made this branch mistake, and tell me what other frustrating Git situations you want me to cover next.

videos.title: The Most Underrated JavaScript Framework Nobody is Talking About!
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction & The Story of Adonis
01:23 The State of JavaScript Frameworks
02:30 What is AdonisJS?
05:00 Built-in Features & Ecosystem
06:28 Exploring the AdonisJS Documentation
09:07 Database Management & Lucid ORM
09:48 Authentication & Social Login (Ally)
12:30 AdonisJS Folder Structure
13:34 Final Thoughts & Call to Action

In this video, we dive deep into one of the most underrated frameworks in the JavaScript and TypeScript ecosystem: AdonisJS. While the tech world is constantly buzzing with discussions about Next.js, NestJS, and Express, AdonisJS quietly offers a robust, fully-featured, and highly developer-friendly experience that often goes unnoticed. Join me as I explore what makes AdonisJS a hidden gem for backend development and why it deserves more attention from the community.

We start with a brief, fascinating story about the origins of the name Adonis and quickly move into a comprehensive overview of the framework. You will discover why AdonisJS is considered a true "batteries-included" framework, coming packed with essential out-of-the-box features like built-in configuration management, routing, controllers, middleware, and body parsing. We also take a hands-on look at its official documentation, exploring how incredibly easy it is to set up a new project, configure your databases, and manage environments from day one.

One of the standout features we discuss is Lucid ORM, AdonisJS's powerful and intuitive database query builder. We also explore its seamless built-in authentication system that supports sessions, API tokens, and even social logins via the Ally package. Whether you are building a robust API server, a server-rendered web application, or a scalable microservice, AdonisJS provides a highly structured and secure foundation.

Are you tired of wiring up every single package manually in your Node.js applications? Watch this video to find out if AdonisJS is the right choice for your next big project. Let me know in the comments if you want a complete, step-by-step hands-on tutorial series on building an application with AdonisJS!

videos.title: C10K Problem Explained in Hindi | System Design | Apache, Nginx, Node.js & BEAM
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction to System Design & C10K
01:35 ChaiCode System Design Cohort
02:03 What exactly is the C10K Problem?
04:05 The Challenge: Apache & One Thread Per Connection
05:30 The Memory Bottleneck (Why you needed 80GB RAM)
06:30 Understanding Blocking I/O Operations
08:45 The Solution: Modern Async I/O & Event Driven Architecture
10:00 How Nginx & Node.js use the Event Loop
14:00 The BEAM Runtime Paradigm (Erlang/Elixir & WhatsApp)
17:20 Summary & Conclusion

Have you ever wondered how modern applications handle thousands of users at the exact same time? Welcome to Chai aur Code! In today's video, we dive deep into the C10K problem—a classic System Design challenge that revolutionized backend engineering. We discuss what it takes to handle 10,000 concurrent connections on a single server and why traditional web servers like Apache struggled to keep up.

We break down the memory bottlenecks and blocking I/O issues of the 'one thread per connection' model and explore how the web evolved to solve this. Discover how modern technologies like Nginx and Node.js use Asynchronous I/O and the Event Loop to efficiently manage massive concurrency. Plus, we take a special look at the BEAM runtime (used by Erlang/Elixir), which powers ultra-scalable systems like WhatsApp and Discord with its unique, OS-independent process scheduler.

Whether you are a beginner in backend development or preparing for System Design interviews, this video will give you a solid foundation in concurrency and server architecture.

videos.title: 3 pillars of software Development for beginners
videos.description:
check out our Udemy courses and cohort at https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction to Software Building Pillars
01:26 Building Software vs. Computer Science
02:16 Topic 1: Understanding the Software Lifecycle
07:15 Topic 2: Defining the Use Case for Your Tech Stack
09:50 Topic 3: The Truth About Trade-offs and Drawbacks
13:04 The Trap of Day 1 Optimization
15:15 Conclusion: Connecting Code to Business

Are you starting your software engineering journey and feeling overwhelmed by all the programming languages, frameworks, and tools out there? In this video, we dive deep into the real-world software development ecosystem and discuss the three most critical pillars every beginner and software developer must know: Software Lifecycle, Use Cases, and Trade-offs.

Often, beginners get stuck focusing solely on learning a programming language, but a language is just a tiny part of building software. We break down why understanding a framework's lifecycle and focusing on business logic is what actually gets products built. Next, we explore how to choose the right tech stack based on real-world use cases, using a chat client as an example. It's not about what's trending; it's about what your business and team actually need.

Finally, we tackle the reality of trade-offs and drawbacks—debunking the hype driven by articles and tutorials, and proving that 'dead' languages like PHP and Ruby on Rails are still powering massive businesses. Plus, we cover why 'Day 1 Optimization' is a trap you need to avoid. If you want to transition from just writing code to actually engineering software and understanding system design, this video is your roadmap. Grab your chai, hit play, and let's demystify the software building process together!

videos.title: The DSA Toolkit
videos.description:
In this video, I explain the step-by-step approach to preparing for data structures and algorithms. I start by mastering the basics of data structures, including linear and non-linear data structures, and then move on to algorithms and interview techniques. I emphasize the importance of understanding CRUD operations and then progressing to more complex techniques like recursion, dynamic programming, and graph algorithms.
https://chaicode.com

00:00 Introduction
02:00 Data Structures Basics
04:00 Algorithms and Interview Techniques
06:00 Step-by-Step Preparation Approach
12:00 Conclusion and Next Steps

videos.title: 100 free seats every month, DSA sheet, Live classes | Coding Hero V2
videos.description:
Ye ha hamara CodingHero V2.
Happy to announce CodingHero V2.
100 free seats every month, DSA sheets, Daily practice problems, weekend quizzes, Live classes by our Heroes. 
If these 100 free users study seriously, 50 more seats on 20th date (otherwise just 100 free seats).
CodingHeroes are getting paid for each classes and we are open for expert paid guest classes (already in talk with few experts).

To keep community engaged with only highly motivated folks, we are charging 299 rs per month as monthly pass (just a superchat). No 6 month or yearly pay. If we add enough value, you can join in next month, otherwise no auto-pay
Twitter pe follow kr le. Hr month ki 1st ko Free Seats ka code announce hoga.

In this video, we'll explore some helpful coding suggestions tailored for those who are just starting their coding journey. Whether you're delving into the world of programming for the first time or looking to enhance your existing skills, these tips are designed to provide you with a solid foundation to build upon. By implementing these strategies, you can streamline your learning process and cultivate a deeper understanding of coding principles.

videos.title: What is JWT token and JWT vs Sessions
videos.description:
https://chaicode.com
In this video, we'll explore the differences between JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and sessions for authentication in web applications. We'll cover how JWTs are stateless, storing all necessary information within the token itself, making them ideal for scalable, distributed systems. On the other hand, sessions store user state on the server, referenced by a session ID, which is sent to the client as a cookie. We'll discuss the pros and cons of each approach, including security implications, storage methods, and typical use cases. By the end of this video, you'll have a clear understanding of when to use JWTs versus sessions in your web development projects.

If you wish, you can write them but we recommend to implement the commands directly.

Then we will get that stream link into our frontend built using React. Using video player, we will learn to handle adaptive streaming of videos:

videos.title: 6 Step guide to master api handling
videos.description:
Text guide: https://chaicode.com/blog/6-step-guide-to-master-api-handling

You have been using APIs in their most basic format. You send a request to a server, and it sends back a response. But APIs can do so much more than that. They can be used to create new APIs, to modify existing APIs, and to integrate APIs with other APIs. Most of the time that you practice is just data picking from APIs or loop through the data. But APIs can do so much more than that. At a max level, you went through the basics of state management.

Visit https://chaicode.com for all related materials, community help, source code etc.
In this video, we will learn about Shadcn. Shadcn is a different take on UI component library where you have the full control of what to have and in what configuration. It sounds little difficult but it is not. This is one of the most easy to use library and goes really well with NextJS. Yes, we will use nextjs in this video. This will be your first introduction as a beginner with nextjs and nextjs routing.

videos.title: Life is not that easy as an entrepreneur
videos.description:
Visit https://chaicode.com for all related materials, community help, source code etc.
This post on linkedIn shocked me from inside as I can totally understand that how difficult it can be to build a business. It's not just about ed-tech but any business.
I have done it offline in 2-3 segments and in online world too. It's not easy and it takes everything out of you. And then comes the folks, who don't know you nor they understand the business and constantly abuse you. 
it's a lot on mental health and is not for everyone.

videos.description:
Visit https://chaicode.com for all related materials, community help, source code etc.
AWS, or Amazon Web Services, is a comprehensive cloud computing platform provided by Amazon. It offers a wide range of services including computing power, storage options, and various other functionalities to help businesses scale and grow. AWS enables users to host dynamic websites, applications, and manage resources efficiently, making it a popular choice for businesses seeking flexible, scalable, and cost-effective cloud solutions.

videos.title: What is an API in Hindi
videos.description:
Visit https://chaicode.com for all related materials, community help, source code etc.
An API, or Application Programming Interface, is a set of rules and protocols for building and interacting with software applications. It's like a bridge that allows different software systems to communicate and share information. For instance, a YouTube API enables developers to integrate YouTube's functionalities, like uploading videos, retrieving comments, or managing playlists, into their own applications or websites. This helps in expanding YouTube's features and services beyond its platform. APIs use standardized requests and responses, making it easier for developers to access and use functionalities of a larger software system like YouTube in a consistent and efficient way.
